title: Gainax copyright change
wikitext:
[]: Gainax copyright change?             Quote Post by ZapX » Sat Oct 04, 2014 12:38 pm tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com')) Looks like the new manga is credited as just Khara rather than Gainax-Khara. Could this mean anything? Just thought some people might be interested.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Oct 04, 2014 12:55 pm ANIMA had a thing since 2009 where the Gainax part of the copyright would disappear and reappear. But then after the July 2010 issue it was only Khara. Maybe there's some ridiculously overcomplicated agreement that's been slowly sorted out?

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Sat Oct 04, 2014 6:56 pm I'd really hate if Khara got hold of the entire license, Gainax should always hold more than them, but then again, who does hold more of the copyright?

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Sat Oct 04, 2014 7:26 pm Maybe this'll be the first step towards us getting an original series DVD/Blu Ray release?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Oct 04, 2014 7:29 pm xPearse wrote: Gainax should always hold more than them

[]:             Quote Post by ThanatosII » Sat Oct 04, 2014 8:00 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Why? The entire creative team behind the original has moved to khara. If anything it makes no sense for Gainax to keep it. Especially when they're just a skeleton company now.

[]:             Quote Post by Jäeger » Sun Oct 05, 2014 3:54 am Ray wrote: Maybe this'll be the first step towards us getting an original series DVD/Blu Ray release?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Oct 05, 2014 4:59 am Jäeger wrote: I don't understand what you mean about getting an original series DVD release

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Sun Oct 05, 2014 9:54 am I really hope that when FINAL comes out we get a "Third Impact Edition" boxset that contains the entire original series (both normal and DC), Death and Rebirth, EoE and all 4 Rebuild movies, all fully remastered and on blu-ray. Also there should be a shit-tonne of extras, like the utterly hilarious "After the End" audio play (with subtitles of course), stuff from the Proposal, interviews with the creative team and so on. Also, the after-credits "silly rant ending" with both Spike Spencer's original version and a new version done in Japanese by Megumi Ogata. And what would really make it great would be if the damn thing came out in the UK. As for the whole Gainax/Khara debacle, I think Khara deserves the rights. The entire team behind Eva are in Khara now, and Gainax basically doesn't exist anymore. Of course what I'd really like is if Khara, Trigger and Gainax alk merged to form a kind of "Neo-Gainax" but that's never happening.

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Sun Oct 05, 2014 1:25 pm The sooner Gainex is out of the picture, the better. This could be because, and I'm hoping, they would want to capitalize on the year 2015 and get a Blu-ray out on NGE. Then Khara can license it out to Funimation, get a re-dub, amongst other things, on EoE and call it a day.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Oct 05, 2014 2:21 pm Just by curiosity, what the hell is that new manga? I saw the images, and it looks like Initial D in the world of Eva with karts and lots of crack injected into it!

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Tue Oct 07, 2014 3:07 pm hui43210 wrote: Then Khara can license it out to Funimation, get a re-dub, amongst other things, on EoE and call it a day.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Oct 07, 2014 4:57 pm I’d just be happy if Funi rereleased the original dub. At this point everyone would be getting what they’ve always wanted anyway. People who have defended the dub for the past near 20 years are gonna hear it, and people who’ve stayed with the original language version won’t have to wait for the delay sthat might result in recording the English dialogue (only to be ordered to halt the release and re-record anyway) all over again. Besides, it’s not like ADV hasn’t already recorded and then re-recorded the English dub of NGE for accuracy’s sake anyway. And even if Funi did re-record the dub, it’s not like it’s gonna result in a Cowboy Bebop quality English dub, so why even bother?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Oct 31, 2014 11:11 am Official Eva site has been update to remove GAINAX from the copyright and now has Eva Production Committee http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-3375.html

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Oct 31, 2014 12:29 pm So, does this mean that GAINAX doesn’t own any parts of the Eva franchise in any fashion anymore? If that’s the case, then since Project Eva is (I think) currently at Khara, does that mean that Anno & Co. now own NGE again? And, of course, does this mean that we might see NGE Blu-rays in the nearer future released by Khara?

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Fri Oct 31, 2014 12:42 pm Pardon my ignorance but would this copyright change mess with the English rights that Funimation has?

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Fri Oct 31, 2014 4:16 pm Dataprime wrote: Pardon my ignorance but would this copyright change mess with the English rights that Funimation has?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Oct 31, 2014 7:10 pm Gainax is dead to me. Despite all the other directors & key management who worked there it was really "The House That Anno Built". Tsurumaki was leading the studio into interesting ground with FLCL & Gunbuster but then he went to work with Anno at Khara. The studio had one last era of relevance with Imaishii delivering magic in Gurren Lagann & Pany & Stocking but once he left there were no key artists in power left. I mean what does Gaianx do now? Do they even have a style anymore or has been all sucked up into that moe blob? If Gainax somehow lost the rights to NGE & it went to Khara then all the better.

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Fri Oct 31, 2014 11:17 pm So, does this mean that when NGE was created, the original copyright was not to Gainax the company, but to the creative team behind it such as Anno and Sadamoto among others. It appears that Gainax, in its early years, was more of an artists and animators collective than a formal production company. Or am I completely mistaken?

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Mon Dec 01, 2014 10:43 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I’d just be happy if Funi rereleased the original dub. At this point everyone would be getting what they’ve always wanted anyway. People who have defended the dub for the past near 20 years are gonna hear it, and people who’ve stayed with the original language version won’t have to wait for the delay sthat might result in recording the English dialogue (only to be ordered to halt the release and re-record anyway) all over again. Besides, it’s not like ADV hasn’t already recorded and then re-recorded the English dub of NGE for accuracy’s sake anyway. And even if Funi did re-record the dub, it’s not like it’s gonna result in a Cowboy Bebop quality English dub, so why even bother?

Could this mean anything? Just thought some people might be interested.
